learningObjectives: Name and use twelve holiday and travel words at Movers A1 level.
Use going to (We're going to visit) to talk about holiday plans.
Use past simple (went, saw, had) to talk about finished holiday events.
Follow a chronological narrative with first, then and finally.
Listen to short stories and understand the order of events.
Write a short message about holiday plans or a past trip (at least 15 words).
Talk about packing, travel and holiday experiences in short spoken answers.

grammarReference.structure: Going to future: We're going to visit
grammarReference.explanation: Use am/is/are + going to + verb to talk about future plans. We're going to visit the beach. Minh is going to pack his suitcase. Are you going to travel by train?
examples: We're going to visit the beach.
Minh is going to pack his suitcase.
They are going to stay at a hotel.
Are you going to travel by train?
commonMistakes: We going to visit (×) → We are going to visit (✓)
Minh is go to pack (×) → Minh is going to pack (✓)
We are going visit (×) → We are going to visit (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use past simple verbs to talk about finished holiday events. Last year Minh went to the mountains. He saw a waterfall. They had fish for lunch.
examples: Last summer they went to the mountains.
They had a picnic on the holiday.
Dad went to the hotel first.
commonMistakes: Minh goed to the beach (×) → Minh went to the beach (✓)
He seed a lake (×) → He saw a lake (✓)
They haved lunch (×) → They had lunch (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Going to = future plans (We're going to visit). Past simple = finished events (We went, we saw, we had). This holiday we're going to the beach. Last year we went to the mountains.
examples: We're going to visit the hotel tomorrow.
Last year we went to the mountains.
Minh is going to pack his suitcase.
Minh saw a waterfall last summer.
commonMistakes: We are going to went (×) → We went / We are going to go (✓)
Last year we are going to visit (×) → Last year we visited / went (✓)
He is going to saw (×) → He is going to see / He saw (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Order Events
lessons.learningObjective: Put holiday events in the correct chronological order using first, then, finally.

exercises.title: Learn: First, Then, Finally
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Choose what happens first.

passage.title: Minh's Family Holiday
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi with his mum and dad. His friend Linh visits before the family holiday.

First, Mum and Dad plan the trip. Dad looks at the map and says, "We're going to visit the beach and the mountains." Minh packs his suitcase. He puts in a map, his passport and the train tickets.

Then the family travels to the station. They take a boat along the river, then get on the train. On the train Minh and Linh look at the map. "We're going to stay at a hotel near the beach," Mum says.

Finally they arrive at the hotel. They swim at the beach and walk in the mountains. Last summer they went to the mountains with Dad. Minh saw a big waterfall and had fish for lunch. It was a wonderful holiday!

questions.questionText: What happens FIRST in the text?
questions.explanation: First, Mum and Dad plan the trip — đoạn đầu.

Minh saw a waterfall last summer
choices.distractorNote: Quá khứ — không phải thứ tự chuyến đi này

questions.questionText: What happens AFTER packing the suitcase?
questions.explanation: Then they travel to the station — sau khi xếp vali.

choices.text: They had fish for lunch last summer

passage.title: Minh's Family Holiday
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi with his mum and dad. His friend Linh visits before the family holiday.

First, Mum and Dad plan the trip. Dad looks at the map and says, "We're going to visit the beach and the mountains." Minh packs his suitcase. He puts in a map, his passport and the train tickets.

Then the family travels to the station. They take a boat along the river, then get on the train. On the train Minh and Linh look at the map. "We're going to stay at a hotel near the beach," Mum says.

Finally they arrive at the hotel. They swim at the beach and walk in the mountains. Last summer they went to the mountains with Dad. Minh saw a big waterfall and had fish for lunch. It was a wonderful holiday!

choices.text: Plan, pack, travel and stay at a hotel

script.title: Packing for the Holiday
script.setting: Minh's bedroom, evening before the trip

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: We're going to visit the beach and the mountains tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Minh, is your suitcase ready? Don't forget your passport.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Yes, Mum. I put my passport and the train tickets in my bag.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Can I help? I have a map of the coast.

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: Good idea. We're going to travel by train after the boat.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: First the boat to the station, then the train to the hotel.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: We're going to stay at a hotel near the beach!

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: That's right. Pack your hat. It's going to be a great holiday.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Holiday Story Sequencing
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to a holiday story and understand the order of events.

exercises.title: Learn: Minh's Holiday Story
exercises.instructions: Listen to Minh tell about last holiday.

script.title: Minh's Holiday Story
script.setting: On the train, telling a story about last summer

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Tell me about your last holiday, Minh.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: OK. First we went to the mountains with Dad.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Then we saw a big waterfall near the hotel.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Wow! What did you do after that?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: We had a picnic by a beautiful lake.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: And finally we went to the beach for two days.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I saw lots of boats on the river too.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: This year we're going to visit the beach again!

script.audioNotes: Chronological holiday story with first/then/finally sequencing. Approx. 50 seconds.

questions.questionText: Where did they go FIRST?

questions.questionText: What did they see THEN?
